target.title: Browser: harden noVNC bootstrap headers
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the loopback-only `/sandbox/novnc` bootstrap page relied on `no-store` and `no-referrer`, but it did not send a document CSP, `nosniff`, or frame-deny headers.
- Why it matters: this page carries the short-lived observer redirect flow, so it should be locked down as tightly as possible even though the route is loopback-only and token-gated.
- What changed: generate a per-response script nonce, attach a restrictive CSP, and add `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` plus `X-Frame-Options: DENY`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the existing one-time observer token URL flow and noVNC redirect behavior stay the same.

2. Request `/sandbox/novnc?token=<valid-token>`.
3. Inspect response headers and bootstrap HTML.

### Expected

- The response includes no-store/no-referrer plus strict document hardening headers.
- The password remains in the URL fragment, not the query string or `Location` header.

### Actual

## Evidence

target.title: fix(feishu): improve external key validation to allow legitimate keys containing '..'
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes #42257 - Feishu image analysis not working

## Problem
The previous implementation rejected any Feishu image/file key containing ".." as a potential path traversal attack. However, legitimate Feishu keys may contain ".." as a substring (e.g., `img_v2_test..key`), causing images to be incorrectly filtered out and preventing agents from analyzing them.

## Root Cause
The security check in `normalizeFeishuExternalKey()` used `includes("..")` which was too broad and blocked legitimate keys containing ".." as a substring.

## Solution
- Refined path traversal detection to only block actual traversal patterns (e.g., `../`, `a/../b`, `..\folder`)
- Split by path separators and check each segment individually
- Allow keys where ".." appears without path separators

## Changes
- `extensions/feishu/src/external-keys.ts`: Improved path traversal detection logic
- `extensions/feishu/src/external-keys.test.ts`: Added regression tests

## Testing
- ✅ All 5 tests pass
- ✅ Verified legitimate keys containing ".." are now accepted
- ✅ Verified actual path traversal attacks are still blocked

## Impact
- Fixes image analysis for Feishu channels
- Maintains security against actual path traversal attacks
- No breaking changes

target.title: feat(image-tool): add imageCompressionQuality and imageCompressionMaxSide config
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #41771

The image tool compresses images automatically before sending them to vision models, but compression parameters were not configurable. This caused detail loss for text-dense documents like contracts and business licenses where high quality matters.

- Add `agents.defaults.imageCompressionQuality` (integer, 1–100): minimum JPEG quality floor. The compression optimizer will not drop below this value. Default: unset (existing ladder floor of 40).
- Add `agents.defaults.imageCompressionMaxSide` (integer, positive): maximum pixel side for the resize ladder. Default: unset (existing ceiling of 2048).

### Example config for document recognition

```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    imageCompressionQuality: 95
    imageCompressionMaxSide: 2000
```

## Changes

- `src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts` — add `imageCompressionQuality` and `imageCompressionMaxSide` to `AgentDefaultsConfig`
- `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts` — add Zod validation for both fields
- `src/config/schema.labels.ts` / `schema.help.ts` — add labels and help text
- `src/web/media.ts` — add `minQuality`/`maxSide` to `WebMediaOptions` and `optimizeImageToJpeg`; thread through `optimizeImageWithFallback` and `loadWebMediaInternal`
- `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts` — read config and pass compression options to `loadWebMedia` calls
- Tests added for `pickCompressionOptions` and `optimizeImageToJpeg` constraint handling

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/tools/image-tool src/web/media` — 60 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm build` — clean build, no warnings
- [x] `pnpm check` — lint clean
- [x] Verify `minQuality` floor is respected: output quality ≥ configured floor
- [x] Verify `maxSide` cap is respected: resizeSide ≤ configured maximum
- [x] Verify fallback to smallest result when constraints prevent hitting size cap

target.title: fix: recover stuck Control UI chat runs
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the Control UI chat can stay stuck in a busy state after a long-running run if the terminal chat event is missed.
- Why it matters: the next user message gets queued in the UI and does not flush automatically, so the session looks stalled until the user pokes it again.
- What changed: added a small client-side watchdog that probes `agent.wait` after chat inactivity and recovers the UI when the gateway already considers the run finished.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not change backend chat protocol, queue semantics, or model rate-limit retry behavior.

## Change Type

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution

target.title: Speed up install smoke Docker builds
target.body:
## Summary

Speed up the `Install Smoke / install-smoke` workflow by skipping the full control UI build only for the smoke Docker image builds.

## What changed

- add `OPENCLAW_SKIP_UI_BUILD` in `Dockerfile`
- when that flag is set, generate a minimal `dist/control-ui/index.html` placeholder instead of running `pnpm ui:build`
- pass `OPENCLAW_SKIP_UI_BUILD=1` only from `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yml` for the root/ext smoke image builds

## Why

The smoke workflow only validates that the Dockerfile still builds and that the CLI starts. It does not exercise the full control UI bundle, so paying the full `pnpm ui:build` cost there is unnecessary.

Release and normal Docker builds are unchanged because the new path is opt-in and only enabled by the smoke workflow.

## Testing

- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check Dockerfile .github/workflows/install-smoke.yml`

target.title: fix(backup): treat non-existent output paths as directories
target.body:
When the user specifies an output directory that doesn't exist yet (without a trailing slash), the backup command was incorrectly treating it as a file path instead of a directory. This caused the archive to be created without the .tar.gz extension.

Fix: Default to treating non-existent paths as directories by checking for archive extensions (.tar.gz, .zip, .tgz). If no extension, treat as directory.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#41830

target.title: Google: show detailed Gemini CLI OAuth extraction failures
target.body:
## Summary

Improve Gemini CLI OAuth credential extraction diagnostics in the active bundled Google plugin.

This PR now targets `extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts` instead of the removed legacy `extensions/google-gemini-cli-auth/oauth.ts` path.

## What changed

- Added extraction diagnostic state alongside the Gemini CLI credential cache.
- Preserved distinct failure details for:
  - Gemini CLI binary missing from `PATH`
  - missing `oauth2.js` or bundled credential source
  - candidate credential files that cannot be parsed for client id/secret
  - unexpected read/extraction exceptions
- Appended those details to the `resolveOAuthClientConfig()` setup error.
- Reset extraction diagnostic state from `clearCredentialsCache()`.
- Added focused regression coverage in `extensions/google/oauth.test.ts` around `resolveOAuthClientConfig()`.

## Why

Current `main` collapses multiple extraction failures into the same generic install-or-set-env-var message. That is misleading when Gemini CLI exists but OpenClaw cannot locate, read, or parse its OAuth client config source.

## Scope

Diagnostics-only. This does not change the automatic Gemini CLI credential extraction behavior.

#54289 may supersede this diagnostics-only fix if maintainers decide to remove or gate automatic Gemini CLI credential extraction.

## Validation

- `pnpm test -- extensions/google/oauth.test.ts`
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts extensions/google/oauth.test.ts`

## AI Assistance

- This PR is AI-assisted (OpenClaw + LLM coding assistant).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Gemini CLI OAuth setup failures should no longer collapse every automatic client-config extraction failure into only the generic install-or-set-env-var guidance; the setup error should include the concrete extraction reason.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 source checkout of this PR branch at `C:\Users\bgmbg\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-pr-41991-proof`, running Node.js v22.22.0 with tsx against the PR implementation.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- Checked out this PR branch in the Windows source checkout at `C:\Users\bgmbg\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-pr-41991-proof`.
- Installed dependencies for the source checkout and ran a live Node/tsx harness against `resolveOAuthClientConfig()`.
- Set `PATH` to `C:\definitely-not-a-real-gemini-path` to simulate an OpenClaw setup where the Gemini CLI binary is unavailable, then called the actual OAuth config resolver from `extensions/google/oauth.credentials.ts`.
- Command: `node --import tsx real-proof.mjs`

Evidence after fix:
```text
COMMAND: node --import tsx real-proof.mjs
CASE: missing Gemini CLI binary in PATH
OUTPUT:
Gemini CLI not found. Install it first: brew install gemini-cli (or npm install -g @google/gemini-cli), or set GEMINI_CLI_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID. Details: Gemini CLI binary was not found in PATH during OAuth credential extraction.
```

Observed result after fix:
The user-facing setup error now preserves the actionable extraction reason after the generic setup guidance: `Details: Gemini CLI binary was not found in PATH during OAuth credential extraction.`

What was not tested:
Full interactive browser OAuth login/token exchange was not exercised for this proof; this PR is limited to setup-error diagnostics when automatic Gemini CLI client-config discovery fails.

target.title: fix(web-fetch): cap error detail for access-denied responses (401/403/407/451)
target.body:
## Problem

When `web_fetch` hits a soft-block HTTP response (401, 403, 407, 451) and Firecrawl is unavailable, the error message includes up to **4,000 characters** of stripped HTML body text. For common login-wall pages (Zhihu, Weibo, Medium, paywalled news sites, etc.), this dumps pages of useless boilerplate into the LLM context:

```
Web fetch failed (403): <<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="...">
SECURITY NOTICE ...
知乎让每一次点击都充满意义 — 如果问题持续存在请登录试一下 ...
(~4000 chars of login-wall marketing copy)
<<<
```

The agent already knows the URL and status code — that is all the information needed to decide the next action. The 4,000-char body content provides **zero** actionable signal for access-denied responses and wastes ~875 tokens per blocked fetch.

## Fix

Introduce a tighter cap (`SOFT_BLOCK_ERROR_MAX_CHARS = 256`) for access-denied status codes (401, 403, 407, 451). Other error statuses (404, 500, etc.) retain the existing 4,000-char limit where the body may contain useful diagnostics.

**Changes:**
- `src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ts`: Add `SOFT_BLOCK_ERROR_MAX_CHARS` / `SOFT_BLOCK_STATUS_CODES` constants; select error cap based on HTTP status in `runWebFetch`.
- `src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts`: 3 new tests covering 403, 401 (short cap), and 500 (full cap preserved).

## Impact

- **Tokens saved**: ~875 per blocked fetch (3,500 chars × ~0.25 tokens/char)
- **No behavioral change** for Firecrawl users (fallback fires before error path)
- **No behavioral change** for non-soft-block errors (404, 500, etc.)
- All existing tests pass (17/17 fetch + 5/5 SSRF)

## Testing

```
vitest run src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts  → 17/17 ✓ (3 new)
vitest run src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ssrf.test.ts   →  5/5  ✓
```

target.body:
## Summary

Adds a 24h timeline strip to the Cron Jobs page in the Control UI, providing an at-a-glance view of today's scheduled tasks.

Closes #40644

## Screenshots

### Dark Theme — Full View
![Full page dark](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dark-full.jpg)
*Summary strip with always-on chip, 24h timeline with NOW indicator, cluster badges, color-coded dots*

### Cluster Hover — Overlapping Jobs
![Cluster hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/cluster-hover.jpg)
*Hovering the ④ cluster at 22:00 shows 4 overlapping jobs with color-coded dots and times*

### Single Dot Hover — Job Info Popup
![Dot hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dot-hover.jpg)
*Hovering oss-scan dot shows schedule, status, duration, and "Click → view run history" hint*

### Light Theme
![Light theme](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/light-theme.jpg)
*Full light theme support via existing CSS variables*

## Features

### Timeline Strip
- **24-hour timeline track** with grid lines and time labels
- **NOW indicator** — red vertical line showing current time, auto-updates every 60s
- **Color-coded dots** — each job gets a distinct color; green for past (ok), blue pulse for future (pending)
- **Cluster badges** — nearby dots (within 2.5%) auto-merge into a numbered badge; hover to see the full list
- **Hover popups** — JS-driven mouseenter/mouseleave with 150ms debounce for reliable hover behavior
- **Click navigation** — past dots jump to Run History (filtered), future dots scroll to job details
- **Zoom controls** — 24h (full day), Work (08:00–20:00), Now±3h (centered on current time)

### Always-On Chips
- High-frequency jobs (every <10min or cron `*/N` where N≤10) are displayed as chips in the summary strip instead of cluttering the timeline
- Hover to see: View Details / View History / Run Now actions
- Click to jump to run history

### Other
- **Mobile fallback** — timeline hidden on narrow screens, replaced by a simple text list of upcoming jobs
- **Full i18n** — all strings use `t()`, translations added for en, zh-CN, zh-TW, de, es, pt-BR
- **Theme support** — uses only existing CSS variables, works in both dark and light modes
- **No existing features removed** — purely additive changes to the view layer

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `ui/src/ui/helpers/cron-timeline.ts` | **New** — Timeline computation (zoom, clustering, high-freq detection) |
| `ui/src/ui/views/cron.ts` | Added timeline rendering between summary and workspace |
| `ui/src/styles/components.css` | Added timeline CSS (523 lines) |
| `ui/src/i18n/locales/*.ts` | Added `cron.timeline.*` keys to all 6 locales |

## Testing

- Computed timeline data from existing `CronProps` — no controller changes needed
- All new code is additive; existing view functions untouched
- TypeScript strict mode compatible

---

*AI Disclosure: This PR was authored with AI assistance (Claude). All code was reviewed and tested by a human.*

target.title: fix: preserve code block indentation in duplicate block collapsing
target.body:
Closes #41699

## Problem

`collapseConsecutiveDuplicateBlocks()` in `src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/errors.ts` uses `block.trim()` when rebuilding text after removing consecutive duplicate blocks. This strips leading whitespace (indentation) from code blocks, making JSON/Python/YAML output harder to read.

## Fix

Replace `result.push(block.trim())` with `result.push(block)`.

The comparison logic already uses a separate `normalizeBlock()` that trims and collapses whitespace for dedup purposes — the output block itself does not need to be trimmed.

## Impact

Only affects messages where consecutive duplicate text blocks exist AND the text contains indented code blocks. The early-return path (`result.length === blocks.length → return text`) is unchanged, so messages without duplicates are not affected at all.

target.title: feat(feishu): add list action and per-agent routing for feishu_chat
target.body:
## Summary
- Add `list` action to the `feishu_chat` tool, enabling agents to list their recent chats (groups and DMs) with pagination and sort support
- Switch from hardcoded first-account client to per-agent account routing via `createFeishuToolClient`, so each agent uses its own bound Feishu account in multi-agent setups
- Make `chat_id` optional (required for `members`/`info`, not needed for `list`)
- Add `accountId` param to allow explicit account override

## Changed files
- `extensions/feishu/src/chat-schema.ts` — extended schema with `list` action, `sort_type`, `user_id_type`, optional `chat_id` and `accountId`
- `extensions/feishu/src/chat.ts` — added `getChatList` handler, switched to `createFeishuToolClient` with per-agent routing
- `extensions/feishu/src/chat.test.ts` — rewrote tests using `createToolFactoryHarness`, added coverage for list action, pagination, error handling, missing `chat_id` validation, and per-agent/explicit account routing

## Test plan
- [x] All 9 unit tests passing (`pnpm test -- extensions/feishu/src/chat.test.ts`)
- [ ] Manual verification in a multi-agent Feishu environment

target.title: fix(codex): deliver native spawn_agent completions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-native `spawn_agent` children can finish successfully, but OpenClaw may not mirror their lifecycle, wake the requester session, or deliver the synthesized parent reply visibly back to Discord.
- Why it matters: Mission Control shows stale running task rows and users have to manually poke the parent agent before the completed child result appears.
- What changed: add a Codex-native subagent monitor, a gateway-side transcript/task reconciler, and a completion delivery adapter that turns native child transcript completions into durable parent completion handoffs.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not replace the broader subagent delivery architecture or implement ACP/native `sessions_spawn` ownership policy; it handles Codex harness native `spawn_agent` child threads and their durable transcript completions.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #44925
- Related #52249
- Related #83352
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex-native `spawn_agent` worker completion now wakes the parent Scout session and produces a visible Discord reply without a manual follow-up ping.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw native install on macOS, gateway LaunchAgent, Discord Mission Control channel, Codex harness native `spawn_agent`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Hot-patched the local installed OpenClaw gateway with the same source behavior in this PR.
  2. Restarted the gateway.
  3. From Discord `#mission-control`, asked the parent agent to spawn a worker to check weather using Codex native `spawn_agent`.
  4. Repeated after earlier failed attempts where the worker completed but the parent only replied after a manual poke.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
San Diego: 63°F and cloudy.

Notable forecast: Monday stays mostly cloudy with a high near 70°F, with SW wind gusts up to 20 mph.

Source: NOAA/NWS weather.gov, KSAN observation station.

End-to-end note: this reply is being sent visibly to <#1493670744029401119> from the subagent completion result.

Sent visibly to #mission-control.

Discord receipt: 1505787418349928589
```

Additional live test after packaging: a longer engineer analysis run completed and proactively returned the full worker response in Discord.

- Observed result after fix: the child worker completed, the parent Scout was re-entered without a user poke, and the final synthesized answer was sent visibly to the Discord Mission Control channel.
- What was not tested: Telegram/Slack/ACP paths, Docker install, and upstream CI before PR creation.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): repeated pre-fix live runs had completed worker results in the parent context, but the parent did not proactively reply until the user sent another Discord message.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Codex native `spawn_agent` owns the child thread lifecycle inside the Codex harness, so OpenClaw was not reliably converting child transcript terminal events into OpenClaw task completion and parent delivery state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no durable gateway-side reconciler scanned Codex child transcripts for `event_msg.task_complete` / `task_failed` and retried parent completion delivery until the parent produced a visible response.
- Contributing context (if known): the old documented `sessions_spawn`/`sessions_yield` flow is not available in the Codex harness path; native `spawn_agent` produces usable child transcript state, but OpenClaw needs an adapter around it.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-monitor.test.ts`
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-notification.test.ts`
  - `src/plugin-sdk/codex-native-task-runtime.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: native Codex child notifications and transcript completions create/mirror task rows, finalize terminal rows, mark delivery pending, wake the requester parent, and only mark delivered after a durable direct parent completion response.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it tests the OpenClaw/Codex adapter seams without needing private Discord credentials or a live Codex websocket.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing subagent announce tests covered generic completion delivery but did not cover Codex-native child transcript reconciliation.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-native delegated workers that complete in the background should now proactively deliver their completion back through the parent session, including visible Discord channel replies, instead of waiting for the user to poke the parent agent.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord parent -> Codex spawn_agent -> child transcript task_complete -> stale OpenClaw task / no proactive visible reply

After:
Discord parent -> Codex spawn_agent -> child transcript task_complete -> OpenClaw reconciler -> parent completion handoff -> visible Discord reply
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: native OpenClaw install, gateway LaunchAgent
- Model/provider: Codex harness, OpenAI Codex native `spawn_agent`
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord `#mission-control`
- Relevant config (redacted): Mission Control Discord channel session bound to Scout/parent agent

### Steps

1. From Discord `#mission-control`, ask the parent agent to spawn a worker using native `spawn_agent`.
2. Let the worker complete without sending a follow-up message.
3. Watch for a parent synthesized reply posted visibly in the same Discord channel.
4. Inspect task rows for `codex-thread:<child>` status and delivery status.

### Expected

- Worker completes.
- OpenClaw finalizes the mirrored `codex-native` task row.
- Parent Scout receives a completion handoff.
- Parent reply is sent visibly to Discord.
- Delivery is marked delivered only after the parent response path succeeds.

### Actual

- The live Discord weather test posted visibly to `#mission-control` with Discord receipt `1505787418349928589`.
- A later long engineer analysis run also completed and returned the full response proactively.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Codex-native worker completion posts a visible Discord parent reply after gateway restart; longer engineer analysis run also completed and proactively returned.
- Edge cases checked: stale terminal rows are not blindly replayed; delivery remains pending when the parent handoff does not produce a durable direct completion response; Discord/channel completion uses the message-tool-only path when required.
- What you did **not** verify: ACP runtime, Docker install, non-Discord channels, and full upstream CI.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: duplicate parent replies if a live notification and transcript reconciliation both observe the same child completion.
  - Mitigation: task delivery state and completion dedupe keys prevent delivered rows from being reconciled again; the reconciler ignores already delivered/failed rows and old terminal rows outside the active/pending window.
- Risk: parent transcript completion exists but external channel delivery still fails.
  - Mitigation: Codex-native completions are treated as agent-mediated completion sources and require durable direct parent completion delivery before marking delivered.

target.title: Render Slack progress drafts as plan cards
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve Slack native plan/task-card rendering for progress mode instead of falling back to legacy section/fields blocks.
- Stream native Slack `plan_update` / `task_update` chunks for progress events, including single-tool and concurrent progress updates.
- Use the newest meaningful progress step as the native plan title when no explicit label is configured, while preserving explicit configured labels.
- Finalize in-flight native progress tasks so completed replies do not leave stale `Running` cards.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --pretty false`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Slack native progress/task-card rendering remains on the native plan path, source-run streams native Slack progress while work is executing, and finalization clears running task state.
Real environment tested: local source-run OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch, Slack workspace `4ok`, channel `C0ASNARACMT`, infra-engineer-originated message mentioning prod Jules; gateway config used `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` through the source-run Codex auth store.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: restarted the local source-run gateway from `/home/simon/Workspace/openclaw-slack-plan-progress-drafts`, verified `/healthz`, then posted an infra-engineer Slack message with marker `native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928` asking Jules to run exactly `python3 -c "import time; [print('progress-step-%d' % i, flush=True) or time.sleep(3) for i in range(1,6)]; print('FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928')"`; then polled `conversations.replies`, source-run session/trajectory files, source-run gateway log, and `/healthz` after completion.
Evidence after fix: copied live output from the source-run Slack Web API/session-log proof:
```text
parent_ts=1778946569.986069
reply_ts=1778946586.604129
marker=FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928
slack_block_type=plan
task_card={"type":"task_card","task_id":"bash_1","title":"Bash — Bash","status":"complete"}
final_seen=true
plan_message_count=1
stale_running_tasks=[]
false_error_tasks=[]
healthz_before={"ok":true,"status":"live"}
healthz_after={"ok":true,"status":"live"}
```
Slack replies for parent ts `1778946569.986069` included one native Slack `plan` block in Jules' reply ts `1778946586.604129`; the plan contained the native task card above; the final reply text contained `FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928`.
Observed result after fix: source-run logs showed Slack inbound for the marker plus `slack-stream` start/appends/stop in channel `C0ASNARACMT` thread `1778946569.986069`; the source-run trajectory ended `status: success` with provider/model `codex/gpt-5.5` / `openai-codex-responses`; no duplicate native progress plan messages were observed, no stale `running`/`in_progress` task remained after completion, and gateway health was `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}` before and after the E2E.
What was not tested: no screenshot/video capture of the transient Slack UI; proof is Slack Web API block state plus source-run gateway logs, session trajectory, and health checks.

target.title: feat(cli-output): tool-use injection + rolling-timer indicator for claude-stream-json backends
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** When Claude calls a tool mid-turn on the `claude-cli` backend, the assistant text stream just pauses — often for tens of seconds on `Read` / `Bash` / `Grep` / `WebFetch`. The end user sees a frozen typing indicator and no signal about which tool is running.
- **Why it matters.** Looks like the gateway hung; users abandon turns that are still in flight. Tool calls live in `stream_event` records, never in `assistant_text` deltas, so the existing assistant-stream consumer never surfaces them.
- **What changed.** Two complementary surfaces on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` (gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`):
  1. **In-stream textual marker** via `onAssistantDelta` — `\n\n[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ ToolName: detail\n` followed by a rolling `_ Ns — HH:MM:SS_` indicator that refreshes every 8s, stripped on the next `text_delta` or on turn end.
  2. **Structured `stream: "tool"` agent event** (`{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}`) via a new optional `onToolEvent` callback — consumed by `cli-runner/execute.ts` and forwarded to the agent event bus for downstream tool-tile UIs.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect({backend, providerId})` — no impact on non-Claude CLI backends. Existing `onAssistantDelta` callers are unaffected. `onToolEvent` is opt-in. No tool-output / tool-result event in this PR (the result is reflected in claude's next `text_delta` and the timer-strip handles UX). Tool args sanitized via `sanitizeToolArgs` before either path.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** tool calls during a Claude turn now produce both an in-stream textual marker (with rolling 8s timer indicator) and a structured `stream: "tool"` agent event, when `backend.jsonlDialect === "claude-stream-json"` or `providerId === "claude-cli"`.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142, Opus 4.7, live OpenClaw gateway driving the `claude-cli` backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`; live Telegram turn that invokes one or more tools (Read / Bash / Grep / WebFetch); observe the textual marker, the 8s tick refresh, and the marker stripping plus `\n\n` separator when assistant text resumes; verify the `stream: "tool"` agent event in `emitAgentEvent` logs.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - Telegram screenshot of a live turn (rolling-timer rendered to the end-user surface): ![telegram rolling-timer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-82285/.proof/telegram-rolling-timer.jpg). Every `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ Bash: …` line in the screenshot is one `emitTool` firing in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` against `claude-cli`.
  - Typecheck clean: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`.
  - The markers are an in-flight overlay delivered via `onAssistantDelta`, not persisted to `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl` (which stores claude's structured `result`) or `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` at default verbosity — Telegram is the canonical capture surface.
- **Observed result after fix:** tool calls render as `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ ToolName: detail` blocks in the assistant text, with the live timer ticking every 8s. When claude resumes streaming text, the timer is stripped and the text continues from a clean `\n\n` boundary. Agent-event consumers receive a structured `{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}` for each tool call.
- **What was not tested:** end-to-end against a dashboard / external listener for the `stream: "tool"` event surface (event is emitted, visible in `emitAgentEvent` logs, but no downstream UI verified beyond that); Linux/macOS execution (developed on Windows only); bundled MCP tool paths (`server_tool_use` / `mcp_tool_use` content-block types are matched in the tracker but I have not driven a live MCP-tool turn through it); focused unit test for `createClaudeToolUseTracker` is not added yet (see Regression Test Plan).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Adds opt-in `onToolEvent` callback on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` + in-stream textual marker over the existing `onAssistantDelta`.
- Overlap to coordinate: #80046 (CLI tool-event bridge). Maintainers' judgment on whether inline rolling markers ride alongside or after the structured bridge.

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The condition this addresses (no tool-call UX during turns) is a property of the Claude CLI JSONL surface (tool calls live in `stream_event`, never in `assistant_text` deltas), not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` — add a `describe("tool-use tracker", …)` block.
- **Scenario locked in:** simulated `stream_event` sequence — `message_start` → `content_block_start (tool_use, name: "Read")` → 3× `content_block_delta (input_json_delta partial)` → `content_block_stop` — asserts the resulting `ClaudeToolEvent` carries the reassembled `args.file_path`, the right `itemId`, and that the parser's `assistantText` now includes the `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ Read: <path>` line.
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode (regression of partial-JSON accumulation, wrong `content_block_stop` dispatch, verbose-gating drift) is fully exercisable by calling the parser's `push()` with a canned JSONL transcript — no live claude needed. Runs in ms.
- **Honest gap:** I have **not** added this test yet. Behaviour is exercised live in my local environment but a frozen-transcript unit test belongs with this PR before flipping out of draft. Flagging as a must-add before un-drafting.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** partial-JSON accumulation in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` could drop or corrupt tool args if `content_block_stop` fires before all `input_json_delta` chunks arrive.
  - **Mitigation:** tracker holds the entry until `content_block_stop`, then parses `entry.input ?? entry.partialJson` via `parseClaudeToolArgs`. The pending unit test (`cli-output.test.ts`) locks in the contract for partial-JSON reassembly.
- **Risk:** rolling-timer `setInterval` leaks if a `content_block_stop` arrives but no subsequent `text_delta` fires (e.g. turn aborts mid-tool).
  - **Mitigation:** `clearToolKeepalive()` is also called on `result`, on `finish()`, and on the next `text_delta`. The parser closure owns the interval handle and explicitly clears it on every terminal path.
- **Risk:** tool args contain sensitive content (file paths, credentials in `WebFetch` URLs, etc.) and surface in both Telegram and agent events.
  - **Mitigation:** `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates and redacts known-sensitive keys before either emission path. Verbose gating (`shouldInjectToolInlineMarkers`) lets sessions opt out of inline markers without losing the structured event.
- **Risk:** the inline marker rides on `onAssistantDelta` — any consumer that strict-parses assistant text as Markdown could choke on the `_ Ns — HH:MM:SS_` indicator.
  - **Mitigation:** marker uses a deliberately conservative `\n\n[…] 🛠️ …\n` shape that's Markdown-safe (Telegram renders it verbatim). Consumers wanting strict text can ignore by subscribing only to the structured `stream: "tool"` event.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** No.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — same allowed-tools set; `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates args before emission.
- **New/changed network calls:** No.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same tools claude already executes; this PR only renders that they're in flight.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — tracker reads `stream_event` records the parser already processes.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`; non-Claude backends unaffected.
- `onToolEvent` is an additive optional callback — existing parser callers don't need to wire it up.
- No new config, no new env vars. Disable by not subscribing to the new event surface.

target.title: fix(gateway): emit session_start hook on agent.send auto-rotation (#83507)
target.body:
Fixes #83507.

`agent.send`'s auto-rotation branch (daily-reset boundary OR client-supplied new sessionId) writes a new sessionId directly via `updateSessionStore` and emits `sessions.changed reason="create"` — but **bypasses** `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook`. Every other session-creation path emits the hook:

- `sessions.reset` → `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts:836`
- `sessions.create` (with `emitCommandHooks=true`) → `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts:1326`
- `/new` and `/reset` slash commands in agent input → same canonical path

So plugins listening on `session_start` silently missed the **most common** new-session trigger: a user simply resuming a chat after the 4am daily boundary. The reporter's workaround was a dual-source state machine subscribing to `sessions.subscribe` and treating `reason="create"` as a synonymous trigger.

## Changes

- `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`: import `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook` from `../session-reset-service.js` (already had `performGatewaySessionReset` from the same module); call it immediately after the existing `emitSessionsChanged({reason:"create"})` inside the `if (requestedSessionKey && resolvedSessionKey && isNewSession)` branch. Argument shape matches the canonical `sessions.reset` site (`cfg`, `sessionKey`, `sessionId`, `storePath`, `sessionFile`, `agentId`).
- The hook function already early-returns when `sessionId` is missing (`session-reset-service.ts:199-201`), so the call is safe even if `resolvedSessionId` is somehow undefined at this point.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Plugins registered for `session_start` were not invoked when `agent.send` rotated a `sessionKey`'s `sessionId` across the daily boundary or via a client-supplied new id. The fix makes the hook fire in lockstep with the existing `sessions.changed reason="create"` event for that branch.

- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 22.x. Probe at `/tmp/probe_83507.mjs` parses the patched `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` and the unchanged `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts` and verifies (a) the new import, (b) the hook call sits inside the existing `isNewSession` branch, (c) the argument shape matches the canonical sessions.reset call site, (d) the hook's existing `sessionId` guard is preserved.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node /tmp/probe_83507.mjs`

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
PASS: emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook imported
PASS: emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook called inside the isNewSession branch
PASS: new hook call passes cfg/sessionKey/sessionId/storePath/sessionFile/agentId
PASS: session-reset-service.ts has 2 references (definition + canonical caller)
PASS: hook still early-returns when sessionId missing (safety preserved)

ALL CASES PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Source-level the new emit site is structurally equivalent to the existing `sessions.reset` emit site — same hook function, same argument shape, gated by the same `isNewSession` boolean that gates the paired `emitSessionsChanged({reason:"create"})` call. Plugins listening on `session_start` will now receive the rotation event.

- **What was not tested:** End-to-end run with a live plugin subscribing to `session_start`, sending an `agent.send` across the daily reset, and observing the hook fire. No live gateway available in this environment. The existing giant `agent.test.ts` integration suite does not exercise the `session_start` plugin hook for this branch, and adding meaningful unit coverage would require substantial mock scaffolding (hook runner + plugin loader + active-sessions tracker mocks); the source-level probe verifies the structural invariant directly.

## Audit (per CLAUDE rules — all 5 steps)

- **Existing-helper check:** Reuses `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook` from `session-reset-service.js` (the canonical hook emitter the reset path already uses). No new function. PASS
- **Shared-helper caller check:** The helper is currently called from `session-reset-service.ts:836` (the reset path) and tested in `drain-active-sessions-for-shutdown.test.ts`. Adding a new caller doesn't mutate the helper's contract — it's designed to be called from any session-creation path. PASS
- **Broader-fix rival scan:** No open PRs touching `agent.ts` auto-rotation / `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook` / `session_start` hook missing-on-rotation. PASS
- **Recent-merge audit:** `git log --oneline -10 -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` shows `91f45d9c8a fix(gateway): dedupe exec followup continuations (#82717)` — unrelated. No recent merges that would conflict. PASS
- **Prototype-pollution scan:** N/A — no external-input key copying.

target.title: fix(codex): unwrap app-server auth token wrappers
target.body:
## Summary

- unwrap JSON token wrapper strings returned from Codex auth profile resolution before calling `account/login/start`
- preserve wrapper-provided ChatGPT account ids for both OAuth and token-backed Codex profiles
- add app-server auth bridge regressions for OAuth wrapper and token-ref wrapper credentials

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed --staged`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server login can receive profile secrets formatted as JSON token wrappers; previously OpenClaw could pass the full JSON blob as `accessToken`, which prevents the Codex app-server from using an otherwise valid ChatGPT auth profile.

Real environment tested: Focused local upstream-branch test seam for the Codex app-server auth bridge. The same downstream fix has also been live-patched and observed working on a local OpenClaw gateway, but this PR branch was not installed over that live gateway.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.test.ts`; `pnpm check:changed --staged`.

Evidence after fix: The new regression tests assert that `account/login/start` receives `accessToken: "wrapped-access-token"` / `"wrapped-ref-access-token"` and `chatgptAccountId` from the wrapper, not the raw serialized JSON wrapper.

Observed result after fix: The focused test file passed with 40 tests, and the staged changed-file gate completed successfully for the extension lane.

What was not tested: I did not install this PR branch over an active real gateway because doing so would disrupt a working local OpenClaw instance; live proof should be rerun by maintainers in a disposable gateway or testbox before landing if required.

target.title: fix(gateway): wire max_completion_tokens/max_tokens through openai-http
target.body:
The `max_completion_tokens` and `max_tokens` parameters were not being forwarded through the OpenAI HTTP API path, causing requests with these parameters to fail or be silently ignored.

This adds parsing and forwarding for both `max_completion_tokens` and `max_tokens` in the `openai-http` gateway handler, aligning with the OpenAI API specification.

target.title: fix(gateway): forward temperature and top_p through OpenAI-compatible HTTP APIs
target.body:
Both `/v1/chat/completions` and `/v1/responses` accept `temperature` and `top_p` in the request body, but the values never reached the upstream provider:

- `openai-http` did not parse them.
- `openresponses-http` schema accepted them as "Phase 1: ignore but accept"; the handler read only `max_output_tokens`.
- `createStreamFnWithExtraParams` forwarded only temperature/maxTokens to the underlying stream function, so even a newly added `AgentStreamParams.topP` would have been silently dropped mid-flight.

Extend `AgentStreamParams` with `topP`, thread it through the runtime stream wrapper, and write `top_p` in both the OpenAI Responses and Chat Completions builders. Add `top_p` to `OPENAI_CODEX_RESPONSES_UNSUPPORTED_PARAMS` so the ChatGPT-based Codex Responses backend strips it server-side, matching how temperature is already handled.

Fixes #81274. Refs #65345.

Changes include:

- Thread `responseFormat` through `AgentStreamParams` and `extra-params` pipeline
- Handle structured output constraints in the runtime stream wrapper
- Test coverage for both chat completions and responses endpoints

target.title: fix(memory): skip placeholder short-term promotions
target.body:
Summary
- Add a shared short-term promotion guard for markdown placeholder/skeleton snippets.
- Apply it when loading the recall store, recording recall/grounded candidates, ranking, relocating, applying, and rehydrating promotion candidates.
- Add regression coverage for marker-only snippets, heading-plus-empty-bullet skeletons, normalized marker-plus-heading placeholders, stale stored placeholders, and direct apply rejection while preserving ordinary short bullet content.

Fixes #80582.

Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts -- --runInBand
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts
- git diff --check

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Short-term promotion could treat markdown placeholders like `-`, heading-plus-empty-bullet skeletons, and previously persisted normalized skeleton snippets like `- ## Entscheidungen` as durable memory candidates, allowing empty daily-note scaffolding to rank and eventually append to `MEMORY.md`.

Real environment tested: Local filesystem-backed memory-core promotion fixtures on macOS, using the repo Vitest wrapper. The tests create real temp workspace files under `memory/`, write the short-term recall store, call the production record/rank/apply functions, and inspect the resulting `MEMORY.md` behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts -- --runInBand`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: The focused regression tests pass: a grounded candidate whose snippet is `-` records no promotable candidate, an already-stored normalized placeholder entry whose snippet is `- ## Entscheidungen` does not rank, and a direct apply attempt with that snippet does not create `MEMORY.md`. The full memory-core dreaming/promotion proof passed with 2 test files and 98 tests.

Observed result after fix: Placeholder snippets are rejected at record, store-load, rank, relocation, apply, and rehydration boundaries. A real bullet containing durable text, `- Keep gateway restarts supervised.`, remains promotable.

What was not tested: I did not run a full live dreaming cron cycle against a private real workspace; the user-reported failure shape is covered with filesystem-backed promotion fixtures rather than private memory contents.

target.title: feat(sessions): archive transcripts and create handoff on model change
target.body:
## Summary

- When the default model changes via config reload, old session transcripts can pollute the new model's behavior through in-context learning (e.g. a broken model's tool-avoidance habits carry over to the replacement model)
- Instead of only clearing model metadata fields (`clearSessionModelFields`), this PR archives old transcripts and creates a structured **handoff file** containing only the user's recent messages
- On the new session's first turn, the handoff context is injected into the reset prompt so the new model knows what the user was working on — without inheriting any behavioral patterns from the old model

## Problem

When switching models (e.g. from MiniMax-Text-01 to Kimi K2.5), the old model's conversation history remains in the `.jsonl` transcript. The new model loads this history and copies the old model's patterns via in-context learning. For example, if the old model fabricated tool outputs instead of calling tools, the new model would do the same — even though it is fully capable of tool calling.

Previously, `clearSessionModelFields()` only cleared the `model`/`modelProvider` metadata but left the transcript intact.

## Solution: Context-Preserving Model Handoff

```
Config detects model change
  -> extractUserMessagesFromTranscript()  // only user messages, no assistant patterns
  -> createModelHandoff()                 // write handoff-<key>.json
  -> archiveSessionTranscripts()          // old .jsonl -> .jsonl.reset.<timestamp>
  -> assign new sessionId                 // new model starts with clean session

Next user message (isNewSession = true)
  -> consumeModelHandoff()               // read + delete handoff file
  -> buildHandoffPromptSection()          // inject user context into reset prompt
```

Key design principle: **trust user messages, do not trust old model responses.** User messages carry intent; assistant responses carry behavioral patterns. Only the former should cross model boundaries.

## Changes

- **`src/config/sessions/model-handoff.ts`** (new): Core handoff logic — extract user messages, create/read/consume handoff files, build prompt section, cleanup old handoffs
- **`src/config/sessions/model-handoff.test.ts`** (new): Tests for all handoff functions
- **`src/config/sessions/store.ts`**: New `archiveAndHandoffOnModelChange()` function that combines archiving + handoff creation + session ID rotation
- **`src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts`**: `resetSessionModels` block now calls `archiveAndHandoffOnModelChange()` with fallback to `clearSessionModelFields()`
- **`src/auto-reply/reply/session-reset-prompt.ts`**: New `buildHandoffAwareResetPrompt()` that injects handoff context on first turn
- **`src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts`**: Use `buildHandoffAwareResetPrompt` instead of `buildBareSessionResetPrompt` for bare session resets

## Handoff file format

```json
{
  "switchedAt": "2026-03-10T14:30:00Z",
  "previousModel": "minimax/MiniMax-Text-01",
  "newModel": "moonshot/kimi-k2.5",
  "sessionKey": "telegram:381198032",
  "recentUserMessages": [
    "Run the news pipeline",
    "I still have not received the video"
  ],
  "archivePath": "abc123.jsonl.reset.2026-03-10T14-30-00.000Z"
}
```

## What the new model sees on first turn

```
[Model Handoff Notice]
You are taking over from a previous model (minimax/MiniMax-Text-01).
The previous conversation has been archived. The user's recent messages were:

- "Run the news pipeline"
- "I still have not received the video"

If the user refers to earlier work, pick up from where they left off based on their messages above.
Execute tasks using your own approach — do not imitate any patterns from the previous model.
```

## Cleanup

- Handoff files older than 30 days are auto-deleted
- At most 5 handoff files retained per session directory
- Archived transcripts use existing `cleanupArchivedSessionTranscripts()` lifecycle

## Test plan

- [ ] Unit tests pass: `pnpm test -- --run src/config/sessions/model-handoff.test.ts`
- [ ] Verify `extractUserMessagesFromTranscript` only extracts user messages
- [ ] Verify `consumeModelHandoff` reads and deletes handoff file (one-shot)
- [ ] Verify `archiveAndHandoffOnModelChange` skips sessions with explicit model overrides
- [ ] Verify fallback to `clearSessionModelFields` when handoff fails
- [ ] Manual: change default model in config, verify old transcript archived, handoff file created, new session starts clean

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): make wiki_lint tool output path-safe
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `wiki_lint` returned the raw lint result as tool `details`, including an absolute `reportPath`, while the CLI path works normally.
- Why it matters: absolute-path-heavy tool output can interact poorly with tool-result handling and produced `unable to resolve opened file path` in the reported runtime path.
- What changed: the `wiki_lint` tool now renders vault-internal report paths relative to the wiki vault with a defensive formatter, and returns a lightweight details object instead of the raw lint result object.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the underlying `lintMemoryWikiVault()` result shape and CLI behavior are unchanged; CLI callers still receive the absolute report path they can read from disk.

AI-assisted PR: yes. I used OpenClaw/Codex assistance, reviewed the diff, and personally ran the verification below.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83420
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `wiki_lint` tool output should avoid exposing the raw absolute `reportPath` in model-visible text and should not return the raw lint result object as details.
- Real environment tested:
  - Host: Linux 6.8.0-111-generic x64
  - Container: `node:24-bookworm`
  - Source: `origin/main` plus this patch
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -v /tmp/openclaw-83420-main:/repo \
  -w /repo node:24-bookworm \
  bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null && pnpm test:extension memory-wiki'
```

I also ran a direct Docker harness invoking `createWikiLintTool(...).execute(...)` against an isolated test vault with lint warnings.

- Evidence after fix (copied live output):

```text
[test-extension] Running 23 test files for memory-wiki
...
Test Files  23 passed (23)
Tests       118 passed (118)
```

Direct current-head tool output after the patch (`69baeae2888b1e652740c3afbdabf08f4ae84eb3`):

```text
toolText=Issues: 3 total (0 errors, 3 warnings)\nContradictions: 0\nOpen questions: 0\nProvenance gaps: 1\nReport: reports/lint.md
detailsReportPath=reports/lint.md
detailsHasVaultRoot=false
detailsContainsVaultRoot=false
toolTextContainsVaultRoot=false
coreLintReportPathAbsolute=true
coreLintReportPathContainsVaultRoot=true
```

- Observed result after fix: the tool-visible summary reports `reports/lint.md`, `details.reportPath` is also `reports/lint.md`, and the tool details no longer include or serialize the vault root. The underlying lint core result still uses an absolute report path for file-oriented callers. If a report path is not inside the vault, the formatter leaves it unchanged instead of fabricating a misleading relative path.
- What was not tested: I did not reproduce the exact full-runtime `unable to resolve opened file path` failure path in Docker; the direct tool harness reproduced the risky output shape and confirmed the patched output shape.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the patch, the same direct harness returned `Report: /tmp/wiki-lint-repro-vault/reports/lint.md` and `details.reportPath: /tmp/wiki-lint-repro-vault/reports/lint.md`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `createWikiLintTool()` reused the raw `lintMemoryWikiVault()` result directly for both visible text and tool metadata. That raw result is appropriate for CLI/file callers but too file-path-heavy for an agent tool result.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no tool-level test ensuring `wiki_lint` normalizes report paths for tool output.
- Contributing context (if known): the CLI already logs a relative report path internally, but the returned result keeps the absolute path for CLI consumers.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/memory-wiki/src/tool.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `wiki_lint` returns `reports/lint.md` in visible text and `details.reportPath`, without leaking the absolute vault path in the visible summary or details payload, while preserving categorized lint details such as broken wikilinks.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in the tool wrapper, not the underlying linter or CLI path.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`wiki_lint` tool responses now show the lint report path relative to the vault (`reports/lint.md`) instead of an absolute local path. CLI lint output is unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
wiki_lint tool -> raw lint result -> absolute reportPath in text/details

After:
wiki_lint tool -> normalized tool payload -> relative reportPath in text/details
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.8.0-111-generic x64
- Runtime/container: Docker `node:24-bookworm`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): isolated memory-wiki test vault under `/tmp`

### Steps

1. Create an isolated memory-wiki vault with a lintable synthesis page.
2. Invoke `createWikiLintTool(config).execute(...)`.
3. Inspect the visible text and `details.reportPath`.

### Expected

- Tool text uses `Report: reports/lint.md` for vault-internal reports.
- Tool `details.reportPath` is `reports/lint.md` for vault-internal reports.
- The underlying CLI/linter behavior remains unchanged.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm test:extension memory-wiki` passed in Docker (`23` files, `118` tests).
  - Direct `createWikiLintTool(...).execute(...)` output now uses `reports/lint.md` and omits `vaultRoot` from tool details.
- Edge cases checked:
  - The underlying CLI/linter result shape was left unchanged so existing `lint.test.ts` absolute-path file reads continue to work.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - I did not reproduce the exact full-runtime `unable to resolve opened file path` failure in Docker.

Codex review:

- Ran `codex review --base origin/main` locally as requested by the contribution guide.
- It produced one P1 finding about `scripts/e2e/telegram-user-credential.ts`, but this PR does not touch that file or any credential helper path; the changed files are only `extensions/memory-wiki/src/tool.ts` and `extensions/memory-wiki/src/tool.test.ts`.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: tool consumers that expected an absolute `details.reportPath` may now see a relative path and no longer receive `vaultRoot` in the agent-tool details payload.
  - Mitigation: this is limited to the agent tool wrapper; the CLI/linter result remains unchanged for file-oriented callers, and agent-tool consumers should resolve `reports/lint.md` relative to their existing memory-wiki vault context rather than relying on a model-visible absolute path.

target.title: fix: include gateway plugin commands in TUI autocomplete
target.body: ## Summary Fix TUI slash autocomplete so gateway-connected TUI sessions can suggest plugin-owned slash commands exposed by the running Gateway. Before this change, `openclaw tui` built autocomplete from the local/static slash command list only. Plugin commands such as `/dreaming`, `/phone`, or `/pair` could be present in the Gateway command surface but missing from TUI suggestions. ## Changes - Add Gateway command listing support to the TUI backend through the existing `commands.list` RPC. - Fetch text-scope command entries for the current agent/provider when the TUI is connected to the Gateway. - Merge returned Gateway command aliases into `getSlashCommands(...)` so plugin-owned commands appear in slash autocomplete. - Keep existing built-in/static commands available and add focused coverage for the dynamic command merge path. ## Verification - `COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/corepack PNPM_HOME=/tmp/pnpm node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tui/commands.test.ts src/tui/gateway-chat.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts` - `COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/corepack PNPM_HOME=/tmp/pnpm pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project tsconfig.core.json` - `git diff --check` After updating the fixture to use the existing `/dreaming` plugin command example, the focused TUI command test was re-run: - `COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/corepack PNPM_HOME=/tmp/pnpm node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tui/commands.test.ts --reporter=verbose` - Result: 1 file passed, 9 tests passed ## Real behavior proof Behavior or issue addressed: plugin commands present in the running Gateway command registry did not appear in `openclaw tui` slash autocomplete because the TUI used only its local/static command list. Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on this branch, loopback Gateway, existing plugin command surface exposed through `commands.list`. Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Started the patched `openclaw tui` connected to the local Gateway. 2. Confirmed the running Gateway exposes the existing plugin-owned `/dreaming` text command: ```bash openclaw gateway call commands.list --params '{"scope":"text","includeArgs":false}' --json \ | jq '.commands[] | select(.name == "dreaming" or (.textAliases // [] | index("/dreaming")))' ``` 3. Typed the `/dreaming` command prefix in the patched TUI autocomplete field. Evidence after fix: The Gateway returned the existing `/dreaming` plugin command: ```json { "name": "dreaming", "nativeName": "dreaming", "textAliases": [ "/dreaming" ], "description": "Enable or disable memory dreaming.", "source": "plugin", "scope": "both", "acceptsArgs": true } ``` Patched TUI screenshot showing `/dreaming` in slash autocomplete: Observed result after fix: gateway-connected TUI autocomplete includes dynamic text command aliases from the Gateway command surface, including plugin-owned commands such as `/dreaming`. What was not tested: No

target.body:
## Summary
- include channel model overrides when collecting configured provider ids for plugin install repair
- keep provider catalog matching unchanged once provider ids are collected
- add regression coverage for providers only referenced through `channels.modelByChannel`

Fixes #83288.

## Real behavior proof

![PR 83328 before/after proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83328-fresh/pr-83328/pr-83328-fix-doctor-include-channel-model-provider-repair-before-after-proof.png)

Fresh deterministic proof artifacts:
- Summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83328-fresh/pr-83328/summary.txt
- Before/source proof log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83328-fresh/pr-83328/before-source-proof.log
- After/PR proof log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83328-fresh/pr-83328/after-pr-83328.log

- Behavior or issue addressed: configured plugin install repair should include provider ids that only appear in channel-scoped model overrides.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu OpenClaw worktree at `/root/src/openclaw-branches/fix-channel-model-provider-repair-83288`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts` and `pnpm check:changed`.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):

```text
RUN  v4.1.6 /root/src/openclaw-branches/fix-channel-model-provider-repair-83288

✓ commands  ../../src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts (16 tests) 839ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  16 passed (16)

[check:changed] src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts: core test
[check:changed] src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.ts: core production
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

- Observed result after fix: a config whose only Anthropic model reference is `channels.modelByChannel.discord.default = "anthropic/claude-opus-4-7"` collects `anthropic-provider` from the provider install catalog.
- What was not tested: a live doctor repair installing a real provider plugin package.
- Before evidence (terminal/code capture from `origin/main`):

```text
src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.ts before this patch explicitly excluded channel overrides:
  for (const { value } of collectConfiguredModelRefs(cfg, {
    includeChannelModelOverrides: false,
  })) {

A provider used only in channels.modelByChannel was therefore invisible to configured provider repair.
```

## Root Cause
- Root cause: provider id collection opted out of channel model override scanning even though those overrides can be the only configured use of a provider.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression test covered provider catalog repair from `channels.modelByChannel` model refs.
- Contributing context: `collectConfiguredModelRefs` already supports channel overrides by default, but this caller disabled them.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this: unit test.
- Target test or file: `src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: a channel-only model override contributes its provider id to configured plugin install repair.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is isolated to provider-id collection before repair/install execution.

## Validation
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve raw external session aliases
target.body:
Closes #63005.

## Behavior addressed

External raw session keys such as `conversation:pair:...` should survive gateway session-key canonicalization. A nested `sessions_send` or agent-scoped lookup should resolve to the same stored session instead of splitting history across raw and `agent:<id>:` aliases.

## Root cause

`migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey` canonicalized raw external keys before ensuring the raw-key store entry was copied to the canonical agent key. The first fix preserved that raw alias, but the mutation lifecycle still replaced or deleted only the canonical `primaryKey`. That left the raw alias stale after `sessions.patch`, `sessions.delete`, `sessions.reset`, agent writeback, and node-event store touches.

## Fix

The migration helper now returns explicit `preservedAliasKeys`. Shared store helpers synchronize those aliases on writes and remove them on delete. The mutation paths that replace session entries now use the shared write helper, and `sessions.delete` removes both canonical and preserved raw aliases. Preserved raw aliases stay internal-only for lookup continuity: `sessions.list` filtering/search/count collapses a raw+canonical alias pair into one visible canonical session row.

Regression coverage now includes:

- raw external migrate/write/delete helper behavior in `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- `sessions.patch` and `sessions.delete` raw-alias lifecycle coverage in `src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts`
- `sessions.reset` raw-alias lifecycle coverage in `src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts`
- raw+canonical alias collapse coverage for list/search/count in `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- resolver visibility contract coverage in `src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts`, covering the stale mock that failed CI on an earlier head
- node-event runtime mock coverage for the new shared write helper in `src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Raw external session aliases are preserved during canonicalization, stay synchronized when the canonical entry is replaced or deleted, and do not duplicate visible list/search/count rows.

**Real environment tested:** PR head `1f5c8069e0c13650011bc78fdd820e33253941f3`, rebased onto `upstream/main` `9657b8e8ce4476cfe0fbb5e375743309dd72750b`; source checkout with Node/tsx for the targeted proof command.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
env OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<minimal-temp-config> OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<empty-temp-state-dir> node --import tsx -e 'const { migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey, writeGatewaySessionStoreEntry, deleteGatewaySessionStoreEntry, listSessionsFromStore } = await import("./src/gateway/session-utils.ts"); const cfg = { session: { mainKey: "main" }, agents: { list: [{ id: "main", default: true }], defaults: { model: { primary: "openai/gpt-5.4" } } } }; const rawKey = "conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"; const canonicalKey = `agent:main:${rawKey}`; const store = { [rawKey]: { sessionId: "sess-raw", updatedAt: 1, subject: "Alias Needle" } }; const migrated = migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey({ cfg, key: rawKey, store }); writeGatewaySessionStoreEntry({ store, primaryKey: migrated.primaryKey, aliasKeys: migrated.preservedAliasKeys, entry: { sessionId: "sess-write", updatedAt: 2, sendPolicy: "deny", subject: "Alias Needle" } }); const listed = listSessionsFromStore({ cfg, storePath: "", store, opts: {} }); const searched = listSessionsFromStore({ cfg, storePath: "", store, opts: { search: "alias needle" } }); const afterWrite = { canonicalSessionId: store[canonicalKey]?.sessionId ?? null, rawSessionId: store[rawKey]?.sessionId ?? null, rawSendPolicy: store[rawKey]?.sendPolicy ?? null, sameEntry: store[canonicalKey] === store[rawKey] }; const visible = { listKeys: listed.sessions.map((session) => session.key), listCount: listed.count, listTotalCount: listed.totalCount, searchKeys: searched.sessions.map((session) => session.key), searchCount: searched.count, searchTotalCount: searched.totalCount }; const deleted = deleteGatewaySessionStoreEntry({ store, primaryKey: migrated.primaryKey, aliasKeys: migrated.preservedAliasKeys }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ primaryKey: migrated.primaryKey, preservedAliasKeys: migrated.preservedAliasKeys, afterWrite, visible, deleted, afterDelete: { canonicalSessionId: store[canonicalKey]?.sessionId ?? null, rawSessionId: store[rawKey]?.sessionId ?? null } }, null, 2));'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the direct after-fix command:

```json
{
  "primaryKey": "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123",
  "preservedAliasKeys": [
    "conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
  ],
  "afterWrite": {
    "canonicalSessionId": "sess-write",
    "rawSessionId": "sess-write",
    "rawSendPolicy": "deny",
    "sameEntry": true
  },
  "visible": {
    "listKeys": [
      "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
    ],
    "listCount": 1,
    "listTotalCount": 1,
    "searchKeys": [
      "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
    ],
    "searchCount": 1,
    "searchTotalCount": 1
  },
  "deleted": true,
  "afterDelete": {
    "canonicalSessionId": null,
    "rawSessionId": null
  }
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The raw alias and canonical key share the same written entry after replacement, including the patched `sendPolicy`; list and search both return one visible canonical row with `totalCount: 1`; delete removes both keys. The targeted server tests also cover `sessions.patch`, `sessions.delete`, and `sessions.reset` against the real gateway handlers.

**What was not tested:** I did not run a full live chat-channel round trip for this backend store migration. There is no visual UI surface for this fix, so screenshots are not applicable.

Additional checks after the latest rebase:

- `pnpm docs:list` ran before editing.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts` passed: `Test Files 11 passed (11)`, `Tests 546 passed (546)`.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.runtime.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts` passed.
- Broad Testbox changed gate was not run because the `blacksmith` CLI is not installed in this environment; `pnpm changed:lanes --json` expanded beyond this PR, so I did not run the broad local gate.

target.title: fix(readme): hide default clawtributor avatars
target.body:
## Summary

- Hide two default-avatar clawtributors from the visible README wall so their 420x420 GitHub default PNGs no longer render larger than normal avatars.
- Keep the existing generated README convention by adding them to the hidden default-avatar cache instead of introducing hand-sized image HTML.
- AI-assisted: prepared with Nex; I verified the diff and proof output locally.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: README clawtributors entries for `constansino` and `18-RAJAT` rendered oversized because their GitHub default avatar URLs return 420x420 PNGs despite the `s=48` query parameter.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, rebased onto current `openclaw/openclaw:main`, with the patched `README.md` from this PR branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local proof script that fetched the two reported avatar URLs, read PNG dimensions from the returned bytes, then parsed the patched README clawtributors block to confirm the oversized avatar URLs/profiles were absent from the visible wall and the normalized logins were present in the hidden default-avatar cache.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the local proof script:

```text
constansino: avatar image/png 1516 bytes 420x420
constansino: visible-url-present=False visible-profile-present=False hidden-present=True
18-rajat: avatar image/png 1560 bytes 420x420
18-rajat: visible-url-present=False visible-profile-present=False hidden-present=True
proof: PASS
```

Observed result after fix: The two target 420x420 default-avatar URLs are no longer present in the visible README clawtributors wall, and `18-rajat` / `constansino` are present in the hidden default-avatar cache that the existing generator convention uses for default avatars.

What was not tested: None

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83628
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the two reported clawtributor entries use GitHub default avatar URLs that ignore the requested `s=48` size and return 420x420 PNGs. The generated README Markdown image syntax does not set explicit width/height, so those default avatars can render oversized compared with normal 48px avatars.
- Missing detection / guardrail: these two logins were not present in the existing `clawtributors:hidden` default-avatar cache.
- Contributing context: `scripts/update-clawtributors.ts` already has a default-avatar hiding convention, so this patch follows that generated README shape instead of adding hand-sized HTML or binary avatar assets.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [x] Existing coverage already sufficient
